howItWorks.intro: This blood alcohol elimination calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: BAC = (Alcohol in grams ÷ (Body Weight kg × r × 10)) - (0.015 × hours drinking × 0.5)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How fast does the body eliminate alcohol?
faq.answer: The average person eliminates alcohol at about 0.015% BAC per hour (roughly one standard drink per hour). This rate is relatively constant and cannot be sped up by coffee, food, or cold showers.

faq.question: What is a standard drink?
faq.answer: One standard drink contains 14 grams of pure alcohol: 12 oz beer (5%), 5 oz wine (12%), or 1.5 oz spirits (40%). Craft beers, large glasses, and strong cocktails may count as 1.5-3 standard drinks.

faq.question: Why do women metabolize alcohol differently?
faq.answer: Women typically have a lower water-to-body-weight ratio (r=0.55 vs 0.68 for men) and less of the enzyme alcohol dehydrogenase. This means the same number of drinks produces a higher BAC in women.
